title: How to Keep Insurance Low on a Cheap Car
metaDescription: Looking for ways to keep insurance low on a cheap car? This guide from BuyAndScrap.com offers honest tips for UK drivers to save money on their policy.
targetKeyword: how to keep insurance low on a cheap car
content:
## How to Keep Insurance Low on a Cheap Car

Found a brilliant bargain on a used car through BuyAndScrap.com? Great! But then the insurance quote hits. Don't despair! It is possible to **keep insurance low on a cheap car** with smart strategies. At BuyAndScrap.com, we want your savings to extend beyond the purchase. Here's our honest, helpful advice for UK drivers.

### Understanding UK Car Insurance Costs

Why's insurance so pricey? UK insurers weigh many factors: age, location, driving history, job, and the car itself. While some are fixed, you can influence the final price, especially when considering **how to keep insurance low on a cheap car**.

### Smart Choices Before You Buy

Big savings often begin before you even buy. Your choice of car is key.

#### Pick a Low Insurance Group Car

Every UK car has an insurance group (1-50); Group 1 is cheapest. Prioritise low-group models when hunting for a budget car. Think Ford Ka, older Vauxhall Corsa, Dacia Sandero, or smaller engine VW Polos/Skoda Fabias. A quick online check before you buy can save hundreds.

#### Engine Size Matters

Smaller engines (lower horsepower) generally mean lower premiums. A 1.0L or 1.2L will almost always be cheaper to insure than a larger version. Less 'zip', more 'cash in your pocket'!

#### Avoid Modifications

Modified cars are almost always more expensive to insure. Spoilers, performance chips, fancy alloy wheels, or custom paint jobs signal higher risk. Stick to standard, factory-spec vehicles for lower premiums. A plain, reliable car is your friend here.

### Driving Habits & Policy Optimisation

Got your budget motor? There are still ways to shave pounds off your policy.

#### Build Your No Claims Discount (NCD)

This is your most powerful tool. Each claim-free year builds your NCD, leading to significant discounts – often 70% or more over time. Drive safely, avoid minor bumps, and watch those savings grow.

#### Increase Your Voluntary Excess

Your excess is the amount you pay towards a claim before your insurer pays out. Add a voluntary one, and agreeing to pay more upfront usually lowers your premium. Just ensure you can afford the total excess if a claim occurs!

#### Pay Annually (If You Can)

Though a lump sum, paying annually almost always beats monthly payments. Insurers often add interest or administration fees to monthly plans, so pay in one go if possible.

#### Restrict Drivers

Only include essential drivers on your policy. Adding young, inexperienced drivers, or those with driving convictions, pushes premiums up. If you're the sole driver, list only yourself.

#### Choose Your Cover Wisely

Options include Third Party Only (TPO), Third Party Fire and Theft (TPFT), and Comprehensive. Counter-intuitively, TPO can sometimes be more expensive than comprehensive for many drivers, as it's often chosen by higher-risk groups. Get quotes for all three; comprehensive often offers the best value and protection.

### Security & Storage

How and where you keep your car impacts your premium.

#### Boost Security Features

Even on a cheap car, approved security features help. An alarm, immobiliser, or a visible steering wheel lock deters thieves and impresses insurers. Make sure you declare any such devices when getting a quote.

#### Where You Park Matters

Parking your car in a locked garage overnight is generally seen as safest, leading to lower premiums. A private driveway is next best, then on-street parking. Be honest about your parking arrangements to avoid invalidating your policy.

### Always Shop Around!

Crucially, never just auto-renew. Use multiple comparison websites and check direct with insurers not listed there. Prices vary wildly; loyalty rarely pays. Getting quotes isn't just about finding a policy; it's about understanding **how to keep insurance low on a cheap car** you've chosen.

### Conclusion

Buying a cheap car from BuyAndScrap.com saves on initial outlay. With these savvy tips, you can extend those savings to your insurance too. You really can **keep insurance low on a cheap car** with research, smart choices, and responsible driving. Don't let high premiums deter you. You're now armed to find that perfect, affordable set of wheels!
